---
title: "Saldo migratorio"
author: "AndrésAndrés Martínez González"
date: "2026-04-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

#pacman::p_load(sf, stringr, dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, reshape2)
pacman::p_load(sf, stringr, dplyr, ggplot2, cowplot)

ruta_data = file.path(".", "data_procesada", "zm")
municipios <- st_read(file.path(ruta_data, 'municipios.gpkg'))
agebs_urbanas <- st_read(file.path(ruta_data, 'agebs_urbanas.gpkg'))
loc_amanzanadas <- st_read(file.path(ruta_data, 'localidades_urbanas_y_rurales_amanzanadas.gpkg'))
```

```{r Lectura de movilidad}
leer_movilidad <- function(tipo = "municipio") {
  n_caracteres_cve <- ifelse(tipo == "municipio", 5, 13)
  
  return(read.csv(file.path(ruta_data, paste0(tipo, "-interno.csv"))) |>
    mutate(# asegurar los caracteres adecuados con ceros a la izquierda
      source = str_pad(as.character(source), width = n_caracteres_cve, pad = "0", side = "left"),
      target = str_pad(as.character(target), width = n_caracteres_cve, pad = "0", side = "left")))
}

movilidad_mun <- leer_movilidad()
head(movilidad_mun)
```

```{r Cálculo de migración}
migracion <- function(df_movilida) {
  return(full_join(
    df_movilida |>
      group_by(cvegeo = source) |>
      summarise(salidas = sum(w), .groups = "drop"),
    df_movilida |>
      group_by(cvegeo = target) |>
      summarise(llegadas = sum(w), .groups = "drop"),
    by = "cvegeo"))
}

migracion_mun <- migracion(movilidad_mun)
head(migracion_mun)

summary(migracion_mun)
```


```{r Unir valores}
ver_coropletas <- function(capa) {
  plot_grid(
    ggplot(capa) +
      geom_sf(aes(fill = salidas), color = NA) +
      guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Expulciones.")) +
      scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fee8c8", high = "#e34a33") +
      theme_void(),
    ggplot(capa) +
      geom_sf(aes(fill = llegadas), color = NA) +
      guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Recibidos.")) +
      scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fee8c8", high = "#e34a33") +
      theme_void() +
      theme(legend.position = "left"),
    ncol=2)
}

ver_coropletas(left_join(municipios, migracion_mun, by = c("CVEGEO" = "cvegeo")))
```

```{r}
movilidad_ageb <- leer_movilidad('ageb')
head(movilidad_ageb)
```

```{r}
migracion_ageb <- migracion(movilidad_ageb)
head(migracion_ageb)
#migracion_ageb

summary(migracion_ageb)
```

```{r}
ver_coropletas(left_join(agebs_urbanas, migracion_ageb, by = c("CVEGEO" = "cvegeo")))
```

```{r}
movilidad_loc <- movilidad_ageb |>
  group_by(source = substr(source, 1, 9), target = substr(target, 1, 9)) |>
  summarise(w = sum(w, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

movilidad_loc
```

```{r}
migracion_loc <- migracion(movilidad_loc)
#head(migracion_loc)
migracion_loc

summary(migracion_loc)
```


## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
